generated_parts.label: Integration Harness + Datum Spine
generated_parts.spec: Integration_harness closure spine with harness_channel, connector_keying, assembly_datum pads, routing clips, service hatch, reciprocal interface labels, inspection checklist marks

requirements: Inter-board harness routing, connectors, assembly datums, connection types and load-path closure
Battery, sensor, actuator, cartridge, payload, cable, or joint modules remain removable without full teardown
Harnesses, tubes, or service channels are routed through visible raceways or protected channels
Access is represented by geometry: hatch, pocket, channel, fastener pattern, slot, window, or removable cover
Every cable exit shows strain relief geometry and routing respects a stated minimum bend radius
Connectors are reachable without disassembling structure, have finger/tool clearance, and mention keying or labeling
Primary locating faces, rail guides, axis rings, spar sockets, or mating pads are visible and labeled
Critical slip, snap, pin, hinge, and slide interfaces state plausible clearances
Each subsystem has at least three adjacent interfaces or datums
Fits are named with standard intent: clearance fit for sliding, transition for locating, interference/press only where justified
Holes, slots, and pockets match real catalog hardware sizes
Material/process, wall assumptions, build direction, and supports are explicit enough for design review
Verify/risk items reference real features or interfaces
Risks cover blocked access, weak interfaces, tolerance mismatch, process limitations, and unsafe overclaiming
Each stated requirement maps to a verification method tied to a named feature or interface
Cross-subsystem interfaces state owner, mating geometry, and what is exchanged
Every inter-part interface names a specific connection type with its DOF and a numeric clearance
Parts can be installed in a stated order where every fastener, connector, and mating feature stays reachable
One primary datum part anchors the assembly; each other part locates off a named parent datum using a 3-2-1 scheme
Every part's primary load traces through named interfaces to the base/ground
Design moves the right direction: fewer interfaces, parts consolidated where service is preserved
Assembly uses the fewest interfaces that still allow service and manufacture
P0 internal_feature_targets appear as part_tree children and geometry_ops ids or targets
Geometry_ops include args.at coordinates distributed across the local subsystem volume
Uses drill, pocket, boss, channel, pattern_polar, fillet, chamfer
Every part_tree child has 4+ dedicated geometry_ops
Uses seed-characteristic ops: chamfer, boss, drill, subtract
Dimensions match cubesat scale: 100x100x340mm
Budget rule: cad_brief.mass_est_g must be a numeric grams value
Joint evidence rule: critical joints need visible insert/washer/locking/preload evidence
Training-quality rule: make part_tree children and geometry_ops mutually traceable by id/name
Analysis-ready rule: verify must include a boundary_conditions object with fixture_points, thermal_zones, and pressure_faces
Assembly sequence rule: cad_brief must include assembly_sequence and disassembly_sequence
Tolerance stack-up rule: for each adjacent_interface in the subsystem plan, include stack_up_mm and fit_class
Dimensional realism rule: all dimensions must be physically plausible for the subsystem scale

verify.value: All required fields are present and valid.
verify.rule: Ensure all fields in the schema are filled out correctly.
Ensure the mass estimate is within design constraints.

verify.rule: Ensure all P0 features are mapped to geometry_ops.

verify.value: All interfaces have reciprocal connection_type, DOF, and clearance_mm.
verify.rule: Ensure all interfaces have reciprocal definitions.

verify.value: All critical joints have visible locking evidence.
verify.rule: Ensure critical joints have visible locking/preload evidence.
Ensure boundary conditions are specified for downstream CFD/FEA.

risk.mit: Review harness routing and consider alternative paths.

risk.mit: Use thermal-resistant fasteners and monitor for loosening.

risk.desc: High vibration during launch may affect connector reliability.
risk.mit: Implement vibration dampening measures and test connectors.

risk.mit: Add strain relief features to all cable exits.

risk.desc: Risk of mis-mating due to lack of keying on some connectors.
risk.mit: Add keying or labeling to all connectors.
